title: Turn stdio back to blocking when command finishes
body:
Fixes: https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues/3152

Sometimes after running buildah run in a terminal, commands executed afterwards which try to read from stdin fail with EAGAIN. This is because it sets O_NONBLOCK on the FD:

[NO TESTS NEEDED] Since I don't know how to test this.

Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>

Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
@nalind I am not sure if this is correct, PTAL?

This assumes the descriptor was originally blocking, and I'm not sure that's a valid assumption.  Also, it looks like we don't set a default value for `options.Logger` in `Run()` in cases where the caller didn't specify one, and the tests are now hitting code paths that attempt to use the value, which they weren't before.
@Vogtinator I can not get the original bug to happen, could you check to see if this fixes your problem?

> @Vogtinator I can not get the original bug to happen, could you check to see if this fixes your problem?

Even by running the loop in #3152? Strange. I'll give it a try, but might take until next week.

> > @Vogtinator I can not get the original bug to happen, could you check to see if this fixes your problem?
> 
> Even by running the loop in #3152? Strange. I'll give it a try, but might take until next week.

Went quicker than expected. I first reproduced the original bug a few times:

```
localhost:~ # grep flags /proc/self/fdinfo/0; while ! grep 'flags.*4002$' /proc/self/fdinfo/0; do buildah run tumbleweed-working-container /bin/true; echo -n .; done; cat -
flags:  02
.......flags:   04002
cat: -: Resource temporarily unavailable
localhost:~ # grep flags /proc/self/fdinfo/0; while ! grep 'flags.*4002$' /proc/self/fdinfo/0; do buildah run tumbleweed-working-container /bin/true; echo -n .; done; cat -
flags:  02
...flags:       04002
cat: -: Resource temporarily unavailable
localhost:~ # grep flags /proc/self/fdinfo/0; while ! grep 'flags.*4002$' /proc/self/fdinfo/0; do buildah run tumbleweed-working-container /bin/true; echo -n .; done; cat -
flags:  02
.....flags:     04002
cat: -: Resource temporarily unavailable
```

and then installed `buildah` from this PR:

```
localhost:~ # grep flags /proc/self/fdinfo/0; while ! grep 'flags.*4002$' /proc/self/fdinfo/0; do buildah run tumbleweed-working-container /bin/true; echo -n .; done; cat -
flags:  02
..............................................................................................................................................................................................................................................................................................................................................................................................................................................................................................................................^C
```

No failure!

For completeness, I also wrote a quick test program which opens `stdin` with `O_NONBLOCK` set and can confirm that it stays set even after multiple invocations of `buildah run`.

From my perspective, LGTM. Thanks!
